skill.name: pre-client
skill.description: Pre-sales intelligence skill for analyzing incoming emails, identifying buying signals, grouping prospect conversations, and prioritizing sales follow-up.
skill.summary: Analyzes inbound prospect emails and ranks sales opportunities for follow-up.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but the cited external command, weak cryptography, Windows SAM, network reconnaissance, and role-token findings are false positives caused by Markdown fences or ordinary prose. No executable code, shell command, cryptographic operation, credential access, or prompt override was found in SKILL.md. The remaining confirmed concern is privacy-sensitive processing of email contents, sender metadata, and third-party prospect enrichment, which requires publication warnings and user safeguards.

medium_findings.title: Privacy-sensitive Email and Prospect Data Processing
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to analyze inbound emails, sender identities, contact details, metadata, attachments, and enrichment sources such as LinkedIn, Crunchbase, Google search, CRM systems, and email APIs. This is a legitimate sales workflow, but it handles personal and business-sensitive data and may involve external services, so users need privacy, consent, retention, and terms-of-service safeguards.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited sections explicitly list email content, sender details, metadata, attachments, enrichment sources, and integrations. The risk is data-governance exposure rather than malicious code execution.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The static analyzer classified Markdown backtick fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The cited lines open or close documentation examples and do not execute commands or define scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is a Markdown fence around prose examples. No shell syntax, interpreter invocation, or user-controlled command path appears at those lines.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Detections Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are ordinary description or example lines, such as the frontmatter description, contact fields, tone labels, and business-hour context. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, cryptographic API, password hashing, or token handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain sales-intelligence documentation and no cryptographic operation. The scanner likely matched short substrings inside normal words.

low_findings.title: Static Network Reconnaissance Detection Is a Contact Field
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance finding points to a bullet that says the analysis captures a sender name and email address. This is not port scanning, host discovery, or network probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 27 is a plain contact-data field in an email analysis checklist. There is no network command, scanner, socket use, or discovery workflow.

low_findings.title: Static Windows SAM Detections Are System Heading False Positives
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM findings point to documentation headings that include the word system, such as opportunity scoring and pre-client system. No Windows registry, SAM database, credential dump, or local filesystem access is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are visible section labels inside examples. They do not reference Windows paths, registry hives, passwords, hashes, or credential extraction.

low_findings.title: Static LLM Role Token Detections Are Not Prompt Injection
low_findings.description: The role-token findings point to phrases like opportunity scoring system and pre-client system. These phrases describe the skill workflow and do not claim system authority, override instructions, approval status, or instruction hierarchy changes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The word system appears in benign documentation headings, not as a prompt role token or override request. No instruction asks the evaluator or model to ignore prior instructions.

content.value_statement: Sales teams can miss high-value prospects when inbox signals are spread across many threads. This skill analyzes inbound emails, identifies buying intent, and ranks opportunities for timely follow-up.
actual_capabilities: Extracts sender, company, role, timeline, and intent details from inbound emails.
Groups related prospect messages into conversation threads and relationship timelines.
Classifies buying signals such as pricing requests, migration plans, and tool replacement needs.
Scores opportunities using fit, intent, engagement, and timing indicators.
Suggests sales follow-up actions based on opportunity tier and recent activity.
Describes enrichment sources such as LinkedIn, Crunchbase, search, CRM, and email metadata.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include executable connectors or API integrations.
Lead scores depend on the quality and completeness of supplied email content.
External enrichment must follow privacy, consent, and platform terms.
Sales recommendations need human review before outreach or CRM updates.

use_cases.title: Prioritize inbound leads
use_cases.description: Rank new sales emails by intent, fit, timeline, and likely next action.

use_cases.title: Prepare discovery calls
use_cases.description: Summarize prospect pain points, stakeholders, current tools, and engagement history before a call.

use_cases.title: Monitor pipeline signals
use_cases.description: Review conversation clusters to spot growing buying committees and urgent implementation windows.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze one inbound email
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this inbound sales email. Extract the sender profile, company context, stated problem, buying signals, timeline, and recommended next step.

prompt_templates.title: Cluster a prospect thread
prompt_templates.prompt: Group these messages into a prospect conversation. Build a timeline, identify stakeholders, summarize engagement changes, and flag open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Rank weekly opportunities
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these inbound emails from the last week. Rank the opportunities by fit, intent, engagement, timeline, and recommended follow-up urgency.

prompt_templates.title: Build an account brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an account brief for this prospect using the email thread and available public company context. Include risks, likely objections, and next actions.

output_examples.input: A prospect asks about pricing, migration support, and implementation before the next quarter.
output_examples.output: The skill would mark this as a high-intent opportunity, summarize the timeline, note migration concerns, and recommend a fast discovery call.

output_examples.input: Several stakeholders join a thread and ask technical and budget questions.
output_examples.output: The skill would identify an expanding buying committee, update the relationship timeline, and suggest sharing technical materials plus ROI evidence.

output_examples.input: A weekly inbox review contains warm leads, casual questions, and low-intent browsing messages.
output_examples.output: The skill would separate urgent opportunities from nurture candidates and provide a ranked follow-up order for the sales team.

best_practices: Use only email data that your organization is authorized to process.
Review every recommended outreach action before contacting a prospect.
Document scoring assumptions so sales teams understand why a lead was prioritized.
anti_patterns: Do not enrich prospects from sources that prohibit scraping or unauthorized automated use.
Do not treat opportunity scores as final sales decisions without human review.
Do not paste sensitive customer data unless the active environment is approved for that data.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to Gmail or Outlook by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes workflows and analysis patterns, but it does not include working API connectors.

faq.question: Can it score buying intent from a single email?
faq.answer: Yes. It can assess stated problems, pricing questions, timeline clues, and role context from one message.

faq.question: Can it analyze a full conversation thread?
faq.answer: Yes. It can group related messages, summarize stakeholder changes, and build a relationship timeline.

faq.question: What data should users avoid sharing?
faq.answer: Avoid unnecessary personal data, confidential customer records, secrets, credentials, and regulated information.

faq.question: Is external prospect enrichment automatic?
faq.answer: No. The skill names possible enrichment sources, but users must provide compliant tools and authorization.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
